mnemonics.title: Fundamental Circuit Formula
mnemonics.content: FCC = m - n + c (Remember: 'My Neighbors' = m, n, c - edges minus vertices plus components)

mnemonics.title: Tree vs Chord
mnemonics.content: TREE has BRANCHES (tree edges), CHORDS connect tree endpoints - BRANCH + CHORD = CIRCUIT

mnemonics.title: Cycle Space Dimension
mnemonics.content: CYCLE SPACE SIZE = 'Fundamental Circuits Form A Basis' - FCFAB

acronyms.usage: FCC = m - n + c = number of fundamental circuits
Dimension of cycle space for connected graph
Binary field used for cycle space vector operations (0 or 1)
Used with fundamental circuits in network analysis

visualTips.visualization: Picture a tree as a 'trunk' and chords as 'branches' sticking out - connecting a branch to the trunk forms a loop (circuit)
Imagine each cycle as a vector - fundamental circuits are basis vectors that can combine (XOR) to form any other cycle
Think of chord as 'bridge' connecting two points on tree - the unique tree path + chord = fundamental circuit
Each fundamental circuit adds one 'new' independent cycle - like each chord opens a new door in the tree structure

analogies.analogy: Like adding one new road (chord) to an existing highway network (tree) - the new road + highway creates one new loop
Fundamental circuits are like basis vectors in linear algebra - they 'span' all possible cycles like basis vectors span a vector space
Tree edges (rank) tell us connectivity, chords (nullity) tell us how many independent loops exist - together they describe the graph completely
A chord is like a 'shortcut' across a tree path - it creates a detour that forms a cycle when combined with the tree path
